title: Graph Theory, Networks, Scheduling, and Routing · Decision Notebook: Graph Representation
summary: Design routes and schedules for deliveries and shared services. Planned artifact: Add and verify the math int graph representation increment in a network routing and scheduling analysis. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Represent vertices, edges, direction, weights, and attributes from a real system.
Pin inputs and environments and separate exploration from rerunnable analysis.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-intermediate cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper
